title: pinus ayacahuite subsp. veitchii
intro: Pinus ayacahuite subsp. veitchii, also known as the Veitch's Mexican pine, is a subspecies of the Pinus ayacahuite species of pine tree native to Mexico. The Veitch's Mexican pine has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. The Veitch's Mexican pine can be prepared as an herbal infusion or decoction, typically made by steeping the needles in hot water, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
